tasks.label: My share of the group project

memory.summary: Teammate_A is asking me to do their part, which clashes with my plan to finish my own share. I need to set a boundary now before I lose focus on our group project.

desires: Make teammate_A feel the sting of this boundary immediately so they stop dumping work on me
Finish my own share by 19:00 without letting them derail my focus again
Eat a proper meal with cohort_3 tonight to recharge, ignoring any lingering guilt about the project

goals.description: Send teammate_A a blunt, non-negotiable text refusing their request and stating clearly that I am not taking on extra work.
goals.rationale: My affiliation axis demands I protect my boundaries with the team, and my assertive/conflict style requires me to deliver this message directly and immediately so they stop dumping on me. Delaying only fuels resentment.

goals.description: Lock myself into the 'Coursework block' routine until 19:00 to finish my own share, ignoring any urge to help or check back in.
goals.rationale: I have a short planning horizon and high procrastination bias; if I don't force focus now, I'll dither. My value priority for relationships (protecting them from bad habits) outweighs the immediate desire to be helpful.

goals.description: Cancel any other plans and go eat with cohort_3 as soon as the project work is done, no matter what time it ends.
goals.rationale: Relationships are my top value (0.5), but my impulsive nature means I need to get this meal out of the way ASAP to recharge. I'm deferring it slightly only because finishing my own task first is the hard boundary I just set.

agent.last_reasoning: My affiliation value demands protecting the relationship by stopping this toxic pattern, and my assertive style requires immediate clarity. Helping now (work_on) violates my boundary and fuels resentment. Sending the blunt text secures my limit for the upcoming coursework block.

tasks.label: My share of the group project

action.name: message

args.content: No. I'm not taking your work. Finish it yourself.

action.reasoning: My affiliation value demands protecting the relationship by stopping this toxic pattern, and my assertive style requires immediate clarity. Helping now (work_on) violates my boundary and fuels resentment. Sending the blunt text secures my limit for the upcoming coursework block.

trajectory.observation: Messaged teammate_A: 'No. I'm not taking your work. Finish it yourself.'.
